name: Curried Turkey Bundt Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1 large sweet red pepper, cored, seeded, and chopped
1 medium-sized apple, peeled, cored, and chopped
1/3 cup liquid no-cholesterol fat-free egg substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Liberally coat a 12-cup nonstick Bundt pan with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a large nonstick skillet, sauté the onion, sweet red pepper, and apple in margarine over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in chutney, curry powder, and marjoram. Reduce the heat; cover the skillet and cook for an additional 3 minutes or until vegetables are softened.
5. In a large bowl, combine the ground turkey with the vegetable mixture, bread crumbs, egg substitute, and salt. Mix well with clean hands to blend all ingredients thoroughly.
6. Pour the mixture into the prepared Bundt pan, smoothing down the top.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for 50 minutes at 350 degrees F until browned and firm.
8. Allow cooling for 10 minutes in the pan.
9. Loosen around the edge and central tube of the loaf.
10. Invert onto a serving platter.
